  Professional Summary
  
    Software engineer with a technical background in environmental, water, and renewable energy systems. I design and build complete platforms end-to-end: from a national health insurance system architected for 54M citizens (28 API endpoints) to county government revenue tools and IoT sensor networks. My approach combines systems thinking with automation at every layer, whether that means architecting multi-package R backends with CI/CD pipelines, automating hardware design validation through 9-stage simulation workflows, or building self-healing data ingestion systems. I work primarily in R, Python, and TypeScript, and I use AI-augmented development as standard daily practice.

title: SmartLicenSync - Revenue Optimizer
text:
Data Science Intern • @iLabAfrica, Strathmore University
  
  A comprehensive business permit and license management platform built for the Kericho County Government - originated from the Innovate254 Hackathon (Aug 2025, Homa Bay County), a 48-hour civic tech challenge organized by @iLabAfrica, Konza Technopolis, and Tom Mboya University. Digitizing own-source revenue collection across 30 wards and 6 sub-counties.
  🔒 Private Repository

title: SmartLicenSync - Revenue Optimizer
text:
Problem
County governments in Kenya face persistent challenges with own-source revenue:

Manual permit processing - paper-based systems prone to errors, fraud, and revenue leakage
No compliance tracking - no way to monitor which businesses have valid permits or are overdue
No spatial visibility - county officials cannot map business distribution or identify underserved wards
Fragmented payments - M-Pesa, bank transfers, and cash payments tracked in separate systems
No audit trail - impossible to trace who approved what, when, and why

Kericho County needed a unified digital platform to replace manual workflows and optimize revenue collection.

title: SmartLicenSync - Revenue Optimizer
text:
Solution
I designed and built a deployment-ready Golem/Shiny application with 11 integrated modules:





flowchart TB
    subgraph UI["Golem/Shiny Application"]
        direction LR
        EXEC["Executive<br/>Dashboard"]
        REG["Business<br/>Registration"]
        PAY["Payment<br/>Processing"]
        COMP["Compliance<br/>Engine"]
        MAP["GIS<br/>Mapping"]
        RPT["Reports &<br/>Analytics"]
    end

    subgraph Auth["Access Control"]
        RBAC["Role-Based<br/>Access Control"]
        AUDIT["Audit Logger<br/>JSONB Snapshots"]
    end

    subgraph Backend["Backend Infrastructure"]
        DB[("PostgreSQL 15<br/>PostGIS")]
        POOL["DBI / pool<br/>Connection Pooling"]
        MIG["Migration<br/>System"]
    end

    subgraph External["External Services"]
        MPESA["M-Pesa<br/>Daraja API"]
        BANK["Bank &<br/>Cash Payments"]
    end

    UI -->|"bs4Dash"| RBAC
    RBAC --> POOL
    POOL --> DB
    MIG --> DB
    PAY -->|"STK Push"| MPESA
    PAY -->|"Receipt"| BANK
    AUDIT -->|"INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE"| DB
    MAP -->|"Leaflet"| DB

    style UI fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#3498db,color:#e0e0e0
    style Auth fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#E84C3D,color:#e0e0e0
    style Backend fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#e0e0e0
    style External fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#f39c12,color:#e0e0e0






Core Modules:

Executive Dashboard - KPI boxes for total revenue, active businesses, compliance rate, growth rate
Business Registration - Full portal with Kenya-specific validations (KRA PIN, phone format, Kericho County GPS bounds)
Payment Integration - M-Pesa, bank transfers, cash, cheque, and online payments with receipt validation
Compliance Engine - Monitors compliant, overdue, expiring, and violated businesses by ward and status
Business Mapping - GIS/geospatial view of business locations via PostGIS + Leaflet
Reports & Analytics - Revenue YTD, monthly averages, growth rate, compliance rate

Platform Architecture:

Golem framework - production-grade Shiny app structure with modular architecture
bs4Dash - Bootstrap 4 admin dashboard UI with sidebar navigation
PostgreSQL 15 with PostGIS - spatially-enabled database, containerized via Docker Compose
Connection pooling via DBI + pool, with migration system, health checks, and backup utilities
Full audit logging - INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE with before/after JSONB snapshots, IP, and user agent tracking
Role-based access control - system, administrative, financial, analytical, and data entry roles

title: SmartLicenSync - Revenue Optimizer
text:
Domain-Specific Design

Built for Kericho County: 30 wards across 6 sub-counties (Kipkelion East/West, Ainamoi, Bureti, Sigowet/Soin, Kericho), with 24 Kenya-specific business categories including duka/kiosk, matatu operations, boda boda, M-Pesa agent, SACCO, tea processing, and dairy/milk collection.


Auto-generated registration numbers: KER{YEAR}{SEQUENCE}
Fee structures from KES 1,000 (small duka) to KES 20,000 (large manufacturing)
M-Pesa receipt validation and callback integration
KRA PIN regex validation (^[A-Z][0-9]{9}[A-Z]$)
Kenyan phone number format (^254[0-9]{9}$)
GPS coordinate bounds locked to Kericho County

title: SmartLicenSync - Revenue Optimizer
text:
Tech Stack

  R
  Golem
  Shiny
  bs4Dash
  PostgreSQL 15
  PostGIS
  DBI / pool
  Leaflet
  M-Pesa API
  Docker Compose
  testthat
  reactable / DT

title: SHA Integrated Health System
text:
Data Science Intern • @iLabAfrica, Strathmore University
  
  A health insurance platform designed and architected for Kenya's Social Health Authority (SHA) - targeting 54 million citizens across 47 counties and 13,000+ health facilities.
  🔒 Private Repository

title: SHA Integrated Health System
text:
Problem
When Kenya transitioned from the National Health Insurance Fund (NHIF) to the Social Health Authority under the Social Health Insurance Act 2023, the new agency inherited critical systemic failures:

Broken member registration - fragmented records across multiple legacy systems
Slow claims processing - 60–90 day payment cycles crippling healthcare providers
Massive fraud losses - estimated at KES 8–10 billion per year
No spatial intelligence - no way to identify coverage gaps or optimize facility distribution across 47 counties

SHA needed a system that could handle three distinct insurance funds (SHIF, PHC, ECCIF), process claims in real time, and detect fraud before payments are made.

title: SHA Integrated Health System
text:
Architecture
I designed a 4-package architecture with clean separation of concerns:





flowchart TB
    subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
        PWA["<b>shaMobile</b><br/>React 18 PWA<br/>Bilingual EN/SW"]
        Dashboard["<b>shaApp</b><br/>Golem/Shiny<br/>9 Role-Gated Modules"]
    end

    subgraph API["API Layer"]
        REST["<b>shaAPI</b><br/>Plumber REST<br/>28 JWT Endpoints"]
        USSD["USSD Access<br/>Feature Phones"]
    end

    subgraph Core["Business Logic"]
        BIZ["<b>shaSystem</b><br/>R Package"]
        CLAIMS["6-Gate Claims<br/>Adjudication"]
        FRAUD["Isolation Forest<br/>Fraud Detection"]
        SPATIAL["Spatial Intelligence<br/>sf / PostGIS"]
    end

    subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
        DB[("PostgreSQL<br/>PostGIS")]
        MPESA["M-Pesa<br/>Payments"]
    end

    PWA -->|"HTTPS"| REST
    Dashboard -->|"HTTPS"| REST
    USSD -->|"HTTPS"| REST
    REST -->|"Delegates"| BIZ
    BIZ --- CLAIMS
    BIZ --- FRAUD
    BIZ --- SPATIAL
    BIZ -->|"DBI/pool"| DB
    BIZ -->|"Callback"| MPESA

    style Frontend fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#3498db,color:#e0e0e0
    style API fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#E84C3D,color:#e0e0e0
    style Core fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#e0e0e0
    style Data fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#f39c12,color:#e0e0e0






1. shaSystem - R Business Logic Package

Pure R package - the only component that touches the database
Member registration with state machine lifecycle management
6-gate claims adjudication engine: schema validation, member eligibility, facility verification, benefit package lookup (ICD-10 code + facility level + fund type), amount ceiling enforcement, and duplicate detection
Isolation Forest ML fraud detection with feedback loop - confirmed fraud upweighted 3x, false positives downweighted 0.5x
Spatial intelligence via sf/PostGIS: nearest facility search, Voronoi catchment computation, county coverage scoring

2. shaAPI - Plumber REST API

28 JWT-authenticated endpoints covering auth, members, claims, anomaly detection, spatial queries, and USSD access
Zero business logic - every route delegates to shaSystem
Idempotency keys, rate limiting, httpOnly cookie authentication
USSD endpoints for feature phone access (critical for rural Kenya)

3. shaApp - Golem/Shiny Dashboard

9 role-gated modules: overview KPIs, registration, claims queue, payments, anomaly case management, analytics (4 leaflet maps + plotly charts), admin, interoperability, and ops
bslib v5 with SHA brand theming
8 RBAC roles with granular permissions

4. shaMobile - React 18 TypeScript PWA

Tailwind CSS, offline-ready with claim drafts
Bilingual (English/Swahili)
Digital health card with QR code
44px touch targets for mobile-first design

title: SHA Integrated Health System
text:
Key Features

Auto-approval pipeline: Outpatient SHIF/PHC claims under KES 5,000 with anomaly score below 0.65 are automatically approved - reducing processing time from weeks to seconds.


ML Fraud Detection: Isolation Forest model scoring claim amount z-scores, facility volume ratios (Poisson-expected), settlement ratios, diagnosis rarity, and member frequency. Batch scoring processes up to 1,000 claims per run.
Spatial Intelligence: PostGIS-backed nearest facility search, population-within-distance analysis, Voronoi catchments for 47 counties using rKenyaCensus polygons.
Kenya-specific design: M-Pesa payment integration, KMHFR facility registry seeding, ICD-10/ICD-11 mapping weighted to Kenya’s disease burden, compliance with Kenya’s Data Protection Act 2019.
Argon2id password hashing via sodium package for secure authentication.

title: SHA Integrated Health System
text:
Tech Stack

  R
  Plumber
  Golem/Shiny
  isotree
  sf / PostGIS
  leaflet
  React 18
  TypeScript
  Tailwind CSS
  PostgreSQL
  JWT / RBAC
  Docker Compose
  nginx
  M-Pesa API

title: About Me
text:
My Story
I’m an engineer who builds complete systems - from hardware sensor nodes to national-scale software platforms. My path started in biomechanical and processing engineering at JKUAT, where I designed an automated irrigation system based on crop water stress index for my undergraduate thesis. That project planted a seed: I saw how combining sensor data with smart decision-making could solve real problems in agriculture and resource management.
After graduating, I spent years in the field - designing solar water pumping systems at Kupate Technologies, then supervising earth dam construction and solar borehole installations across multiple Kenyan counties with the Ministry of Agriculture. These roles taught me what no classroom could: how systems fail in the real world, why design assumptions break under field conditions, and how critical good data infrastructure is for public projects.
That field experience drove me to data science. I taught myself R and Python, earned certifications in machine learning and statistical analysis, and enrolled in an MSc in Environmental and Biosystems Engineering at the University of Nairobi. Today, I bring an engineer’s systems thinking to every software project I build - whether it’s a health insurance platform designed for 54 million citizens or an IoT sensor node that replaces $10,000 lab equipment for under $100.

title: About Me
text:
What I Do Now
As a Data Science Intern at @iLabAfrica Research & Innovation Centre, Strathmore University, I work under Dr. John Olukuru (Head, Data Science and Analytics) on applied research and building end-to-end platforms for institutions that need robust, production-grade systems:

Fraud detection & revenue analytics - applying machine learning to health insurance fraud detection and county government revenue optimization under Dr. Olukuru’s supervision
Civic tech & AI for governance - participated in the Innovate254 Hackathon (Homa Bay County), co-creating AI and cybersecurity solutions for digital public service across Kenya’s 47 counties
National-scale health systems - architecting claims processing, spatial analytics, and ML-driven fraud detection for Kenya’s Social Health Authority
County government infrastructure - digitizing revenue collection, business licensing, and compliance monitoring (SmartLicenSync for Kericho County)
Data science & ML - from Isolation Forest anomaly detection to spatial analytics with PostGIS, always in service of a real decision-making need

I work primarily in R, Python, and TypeScript, and I use AI-augmented development (Claude Code + Quarto + R) as standard daily practice.

title: About Me
section: How I Think About Building Systems
text:
How I Think About Building Systems

  
    Engineer First
    Every system I build starts with understanding the physical, institutional, and human constraints - not just the tech stack.
  
  
    End-to-End Ownership
    From database schema to frontend UX to deployment pipelines - I design and build the whole thing, not just one layer.
  
  
    Context-Driven Design
    Building for Kenya means designing for M-Pesa payments, USSD access on feature phones, and intermittent connectivity.
  
  
    Reproducibility
    Quarto reports, version-controlled pipelines, Docker containers, and automated testing - every output is reproducible.
